title: Pfand
text: Bottles carry a deposit (up to 25 cents). Germans return them at supermarket machines — or leave them beside bins so collectors can have them. Never throw a Pfandflasche away!
phrase: Wo kann ich die Flaschen abgeben?
phraseEn: Where can I return the bottles?

title: Sonntagsruhe
text: Sunday is legally quiet: shops are closed, and drilling, mowing or loud music can get you a neighborly complaint. Plan your groceries on Saturday.

title: Kaffee und Kuchen
text: The German afternoon ritual — coffee and cake around 3–4 pm, especially on weekends with family. Opa's favorite hour of the day.
phraseEn: Will you come over for coffee?

title: Feierabend
text: The sacred moment work ends. It's also a greeting: colleagues say 'Schönen Feierabend!' when leaving. Work-life separation is taken seriously.

title: Brotkultur
text: Germany has over 3,000 registered bread varieties — UNESCO lists German bread culture as intangible heritage. Dinner is often just 'Abendbrot': bread, cheese, cold cuts.

title: Du oder Sie?
text: 'Sie' is for strangers, officials, and colleagues until offered otherwise. Moving to 'du' is a small ceremony — usually the older or senior person offers it.

title: Pünktlichkeit
text: Five minutes early is on time; on time is late. For job interviews and doctor's appointments, arrive early. Trains, ironically, are exempt from this rule.

title: Mülltrennung
text: Waste separation is a national discipline: paper, packaging (yellow bin), bio, glass by color, and Restmüll. Your neighbors WILL notice mistakes.
phraseEn: Which bin does this go in?

title: Vereine
text: Germany runs on clubs — sport, choir, hiking, even rabbit breeding. Joining a Verein is the single best way to make German friends and practice the language.

title: Schultüte
text: On the first day of school, children receive a giant paper cone filled with sweets and gifts. A tradition since the 1800s — ask any German about theirs.
phraseEn: What was in your school cone?

title: Fahrradwege
text: Bike lanes are serious infrastructure. Walking in one earns you an angry bell. Many commute by bike year-round — rain gear included.

title: Anmeldung
text: Within two weeks of moving, you must register your address at the Bürgeramt. This paper (Meldebescheinigung) unlocks everything: bank, SIM, contracts.

title: Kleingarten
text: Those tiny garden colonies at the city edge? Schrebergärten — rented mini-paradises with strict rules about hedge height. Opa has one, of course.

title: Weihnachtsmarkt
text: From late November, town squares fill with wooden stalls, Glühwein and Lebkuchen. Each region has its own specialty — Nürnberg and Dresden are legendary.
phraseEn: A cup of mulled wine, please.
